ab: I Fafokod ko loma’ niyam yo mamang ho kako, micodad to kako i, malinah kami a tala-Taw-yin, itira: sato kami a ma’orip.
ch: 小時候我們家住在東河，上學以後，就搬到了桃園，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Fafokod. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Micodad to i ta’akay picodadan i, minanam kako to cecay nananamen, pakayniay i serangawan no Yin-cu-min, itira a mafana’ to masamaamaanay a serangawan no Yin-cu-min.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Orasaka, aniniay pipahanhanan no picodadan, minokay a talaniyaro’ kako aci kaka^ ako to fa’inayan, miliso’ to mato’asay, mangalay a misapanang to serangawan no niyah.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Falo^ to polo’ ko mihecaan noya mato’asay, nika nengneng ri, tanektek ho^ ko tireng.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Tengilen ako ciira, pina^ a sasowalan ko nikafana’an iso han ako, mafa:na’ to no Pangcah a sowal ciira.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的阿美語說得很流利。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Pangcah.

ab: Mafana:’ to no Tayan, no ngayngay ato no Ripon a sowal.
ch: 也會說泰雅語、客家話和日本話。
en: As well as Atayal, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: Na mitengilan to ako, saka mafana’ kako, hilam o na micodaday ciira i kaRiponan yo mamang ho^.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child.

ab: O Tayan ko fafahi^ nira, ’aloman ko kananaman nira a ngayngay i katayalan. Orasaka, adihay ko kafana’an nira a sowal.
ch: 太太是泰雅族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: His wife is an Atayal, and he knows many [Hakkas] at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Yo mitengil kami i, adihay ko nipainian no mato’asay to pakayniay i fenek to pi’adop.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Ano caaca:ay pakasapi:nang a mitengil i, nika ma-lu-ing niyam i saselican, manga’ay a tomadawen a mitengil.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Yo mamang ho^ kako, caay ka fana’ kako to serangawan no niyah, mangodo^ to ao Pangcah kako sa ko harateng.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Matongal mato:ngal to ko nikafana’ i, mahemek kako ao Pangcah ko tireng.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為阿美族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Pangcah is unique and something to be proud of.
